title: Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits
summary: Practical guidance for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” within “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”. It supports life safety, official alerts, accountability, safe movement, shelter decisions, and controlled recovery, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” within “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”. It supports life safety, official alerts, accountability, safe movement, shelter decisions, and controlled recovery, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Stop condition: Do not re-enter a restricted, burning, smoke-filled, flooded, gas-affected, electrically live, or structurally uncertain area. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Reduced fallback: If “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use the safest verified shelter or route directed by the competent authority.
materials: flashlight and spare power — for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”
weather and official alert source — for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”
utility shutoff information — for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”
smoke and carbon-monoxide alarms where applicable — for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”
marked exit routes and meeting point — for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”
steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Identify the active hazard, official instruction, safest usable zone, exits, utilities, and people needing assistance. Record the result under “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment” before continuing “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits”.

Inspect from a safe position for smoke, heat, water, gas odour, sparks, falling objects, cracks, unstable surfaces, and blocked routes. Recheck this point before closing “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” for “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Move people early to the safer zone or leave when instructed; take medicines, communication, identification, water, and essential mobility aids. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” for “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Control only hazards that can be handled without entering smoke, floodwater, live electricity, gas, fire, or an unstable structure. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits”.

Mark unsafe areas, account for everyone, record changes, and reassess after every aftershock, weather shift, alarm, or utility change. Include the outcome of “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in the handover for “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.
warnings: Do not re-enter a restricted, burning, smoke-filled, flooded, gas-affected, electrically live, or structurally uncertain area. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Generators, grills, and fuel-burning devices must never operate inside homes, garages, or other enclosed occupied spaces. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.
common_mistakes: Skipping the opening control makes “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” unreliable: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Do not re-enter a restricted, burning, smoke-filled, flooded, gas-affected, electrically live, or structurally uncertain area. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Failing to document the final handover action leaves the next person without a trustworthy starting point: Mark unsafe areas, account for everyone, record changes, and reassess after every aftershock, weather shift, alarm, or utility change. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.
alternatives: If “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use the safest verified shelter or route directed by the competent authority.

Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” when the safe zone gains smoke, heat, water, gas odour, electrical signs, falling debris, new cracks, or a blocked exit. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

Stop and reassess “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” when official instructions change, people cannot be accounted for, or anyone develops breathing trouble, confusion, collapse, or injury. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” while the area has smoke, gas odour, rising water, unstable walls or roof, falling objects, blocked exits, extreme heat or cold, or an evacuation order. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.

This card is not authorization to re-enter a restricted structure, shelter in a flood-prone basement, use flame near fuel, or ignore an official evacuation route. Topic-specific stop check for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits”: also follow this warning: “Do not re-enter a restricted, burning, smoke-filled, flooded, gas-affected, electrically live, or structurally uncertain area in “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits”.”. Apply this boundary to “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”.
short_term: Complete one verified cycle for “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”, record its owner, time, result, unresolved risk, and next review.
long_term: Review “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” after real use in “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to the safest verified shelter or route directed by the competent authority.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Shelter checkpoint: structure and fire exits” for “Shelter · Weather · Greece · Apartment” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.
